scores.correctness_notes: The response correctly covers the Loki layer YAML syntax and key fields, but significantly misses the core of the question — explaining ALL protocol options. The gold standard requires coverage of opentelemetry (OTLP) and syslog (RFC 5424) types with their respective location formats and auto-label behaviors. The agent only discusses the Loki protocol in depth and never mentions the other two supported types.
scores.hallucination_notes: Several specific claims are not verifiable from the gold standard and may be fabricated: (1) 'Pebble does not currently support client-certificate authentication' — a specific negative claim with no basis in the gold standard; (2) 'Pebble buffers log entries in a ring buffer (in memory) and retries on transient failures' with 'oldest entries in the buffer are dropped' — very specific internal behavior claims not mentioned in the reference; (3) 'no on-disk persistence' claim. The loki_push_api library reference and charm integration pattern are plausible but the charm code details were not asked for and the library URL specifics are unverifiable.
